calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. ALICE TRAEG FOR THE EXECUTOR NINA COSTANDI; ATTY. MICHAEL BASSI FOR THE OBJECTOR JENNIFER GOLLER.

THE COURT ISSUED A TENTATIVE RULING WITH REGARDS TO THE EXECUTOR'S EXTRAORDINARY FEES WHICH IS 1/2 SPLIT BETWEEN WHAT WOULD BE CONSIDERED ORDINARY AND THAT WHICH WOULD BE EXTRAORDINARY. THIS WOULD RESULT IN $80 BEING REDUCED TO $40 FOR EXTRAORDINARY FEES. THE EXTRAORDINARY FEES OF A. TRAEG IS REDUCED BY $1,000 TO $7,000.00. MS. TRAEG STATED THAT THE EX-PARTE PETITION HAD SERVED ITS PURPOSE AND THE REASON FOR ASKING $80 IN EXTRAORDINARY FEES IS BECAUSE OF THE HIGHER RATE TYPE OF DECISION MAKING IN BEHALF OF ALL BENEFICIARIES IN MEDIATION. MR. BASSI REPORTED TO THE COURT HIS CONCERN REGARDING THE $17,000.00 PERSONAL PROPERTY NOT BEING SOLD AND THAT THE PETITION FOR FINAL DISTIBUTION IS NOT MOVING ALONG. MR. BASSI STATED THAT THERE WAS NOTHING DONE FOR MORE THAN A YEAR, TOO MANY PROBLEMS AND CONFUSION AND STILL NO SOLUTION TO THE PROBLEMS. MS. TRAEG EXPLAINED THERE ARE ONLY 2 MAIN ITEMS LEFT UNSOLD WHICH ARE THE HUMMELL COLLECTION AND THE DEGENHART GLASS.
VINTAGE COLLECTION WAS NOT ABLE TO SELL THEM AT THEIR STORE OR ON THE INTERNET AND HAD LOST THEIR LEASE. THE PARTIES AGREED THAT THE HUMMELL COLLECTION VALUED AT $2,984.00 WOULD BE GIVEN TO THE EXECUTOR, NINA COSTANDI, IN KIND AS HER EXTRAORDINARY FEE PAYMENT. THE $7,000 EXTRAORDINARY FEE OF MS. TAEG IS PAYABLE INTO 2 INSTALLMENTS. THE FIRST $3,500 WOULD BE PAID AT THIS TIME AND THE REMAINING $3,500 IS TO BE PAID UPON COMPLETION OF DISPOSITION OF ALL PERSONAL PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229 Clerk: Cynthia Herbert. Bailiff: Deputy Cruz

Off Calendar Oct-17-2006 Continued To Jan-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Sanh Tran appearing for Jennifer Goller. Alice Traeg appearing for Nina Costandi. Also appearing Thomas Tagliarina.
Mr. Tran request a continuance for discovery purposes. Court is referring this matter to the Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 03, 2007. The matter is continued to January 10, 2007 for status conference.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Susan Lee CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition Fot Declaratory Relief To Determine Whether Proposed Petition Snd Objection Constitute A Contest.
Off Calendar Sep-05-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Michael Bassi appearing for moving party. Alice Traeg appearing for executor. Ms. Traeg is not objecting to the challenge of the codicil. She is objecting to the removal of the executor. Oral argument presented by both counsel. Matter granted. Order signed. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Floyd Butler. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-31-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearences by A. B, Traeg, atty for petitioner, T. Tagalini and S. B. Tran, atty for J. Gollier.
S.B. Tran wishes contest the codicil, but not the will, and is favor of an outside personal representative. He wants to file a safe harbor petition. Court states that the petition will go forward with a bond for $276000.00 and limited powers.
Judge:Dorothy Mc Math, Clerk:Larrisa Dotson, Rptr:Christina Paxton, Baliff:Mitzota

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-03-2006 Continued To Jul-31-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Alice Brown Traeg appearing for petitioner Nina costandi. John Leutza appearing for Jennifer Goller. Mr. Leutza is working with Mr. Bassi. They are considering filing objections to the codicil and appointment of the executor. Matter is continued to 7/31. Ms. Traeg can file an exparte petition for appointment of special administrator and set it on the exparte calendar and notice everyone. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
